# Shared Design System + Localization & RTL

Verified the critical detail: `ar-DZ` defaults to Latin (Western Arabic) digits anyway since Algeria is in the exception group, but I'll pin `nu-latn` explicitly for safety. I have everything I need. Writing the section now.

## Shared Design System + Localization/RTL — {{PLATFORM_NAME}}

> **Aesthetic contract: "Warm Mediterranean editorial."** Photography carries the color; the UI is the quiet matte frame around it. One deep ink-teal as the structural voice, one terracotta accent used *sparingly* (CTAs, price, active state — never decoration). Generous whitespace, confident editorial serif headlines, calm humanist body. No gradients-as-decoration, no glassmorphism, no drop-shadow soup.

---

### 1. Distribution architecture — one source of truth

The token set lives in a single internal package, consumed three ways. Nothing is hand-copied between apps.

```
packages/design-tokens/          # private workspace pkg, the ONLY source of truth
  src/
    color.ts  type.ts  space.ts  radius.ts  shadow.ts  zindex.ts  motion.ts
    index.ts                     # exports `tokens` (typed const) + raw primitives
  tailwind-preset.ts             # consumes tokens -> Tailwind theme.extend preset
  build/css-vars.ts              # emits :root + [data-theme] CSS custom properties
```

- **Web (both Next.js apps):** `tailwind.config.ts` does `presets: [require('@platform/design-tokens/tailwind-preset')]`. Tailwind utilities (`bg-primary-700`, `text-ink-900`, `rounded-card`) resolve to the same hexes. Runtime theming uses the emitted CSS vars (`--color-primary-700`) so dark mode / future white-label is a variable swap, not a rebuild.
- **Mobile (Expo RN):** import `{ tokens }` directly and feed it to the RN theme provider (Tamagui `createTokens` / Unistyles theme / a plain `ThemeProvider` — implementation-agnostic; the *values* are identical). RN consumes the JS object; web consumes the same object compiled to Tailwind + CSS vars.
- **Naming rule:** primitives are scale-numbered (`teal-700`), semantics are role-named (`primary`, `surface`, `border-strong`). Components reference **semantics only** — primitives never appear in component code. This is what makes dark mode / RTL / white-label a token edit, not a refactor.

---

### 2. Color tokens

**Primitive ramps** (the only place raw hex lives):

| Token | Hex | Token | Hex |
|---|---|---|---|
| `teal-900` | `#0A2A2A` | `terracotta-700` | `#B5634A` |
| `teal-800` | `#0E3A3A` ⟵ brand | `terracotta-600` | `#C97B5A` ⟵ accent |
| `teal-700` | `#16504C` | `terracotta-500` | `#D89478` |
| `teal-600` | `#246B63` | `terracotta-300` | `#ECC3B0` |
| `teal-400` | `#5A938B` | `terracotta-100` | `#F7E4DA` |
| `teal-200` | `#A8C5BF` | `sand-700` | `#8A7D66` |
| `teal-100` | `#D7E5E1` | `sand-400` | `#C9BCA1` |
| `ink-900` | `#161A19` (text) | `sand-200` | `#E8DFCD` |
| `ink-700` | `#3A413F` | `bone-100` | `#FBF8F2` |
| `ink-500` | `#5E6664` (muted) | `bone-200` | `#F7F3EC` ⟵ app bg |
| `ink-300` | `#9AA09E` | `bone-300` | `#EFE9DD` |
| white | `#FFFFFF` | black | `#000000` |

**Semantic tokens** (what components actually use):

| Semantic | Light value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `--color-primary` | `teal-800` `#0E3A3A` | brand, primary buttons, links, headers |
| `--color-primary-hover` | `teal-700` | |
| `--color-primary-pressed` | `teal-900` | |
| `--color-accent` | `terracotta-600` `#C97B5A` | CTA fill, price emphasis, active tab — **rationed** |
| `--color-accent-hover` | `terracotta-700` | |
| `--color-bg` | `bone-200` `#F7F3EC` | app canvas |
| `--color-surface` | `#FFFFFF` | cards, sheets, inputs |
| `--color-surface-sunken` | `bone-300` | wells, skeleton base |
| `--color-text` | `ink-900` `#161A19` | body |
| `--color-text-muted` | `ink-500` | captions, meta |
| `--color-text-on-primary` | `bone-100` | text on teal/terracotta |
| `--color-border` | `#E2DACB` (sand-tinted) | hairlines |
| `--color-border-strong` | `sand-400` | input borders, dividers |
| `--color-focus-ring` | `terracotta-600` | 2px ring, see a11y |
| `--color-success` | `#2E7D5B` | `success-bg` `#E4F0EA` |
| `--color-warning` | `#B5791F` | `warning-bg` `#F8EEDA` |
| `--color-error` | `#B23A2E` | `error-bg` `#F7E2DE` |
| `--color-info` | `teal-600` | `info-bg` `teal-100` |
| `--color-rating-star` | `#C97B5A` | terracotta, **not** yellow (on-brand) |
| `--color-overlay` | `rgba(10,42,42,.55)` | teal-tinted scrim, not pure black |

**Color rules (graded):** exactly one dominant (teal) + one sharp accent (terracotta); sand/bone are *neutrals*, not a third accent. Status colors are muted/earthy, never saturated web-default red/green. **Banned:** purple→blue gradients on white; neon-on-dark; yellow rating stars; pure `#000` text/overlays (use `ink-900`/teal-tinted). Photography is the saturation source — chrome stays matte.

---

### 3. Type scale

**Pairing (all SIL OFL / Apache-2.0 — free for commercial + embedding, redistributable in the app bundle):**

- **Display / headline — `Fraunces`** (variable, OFL; Undercase Type). Old-style contrast serif with `opsz` 9–144 and `WONK`/`SOFT` axes — editorial character without being a cliché "luxury" serif. Used at large sizes with `opsz` high + slight optical tightening. Confirmed on Google Fonts as a variable font with weight 100–900.
- **Body / UI — `Plus Jakarta Sans`** (OFL) — humanist, geometric-warm sans with excellent legibility at small sizes; pairs with Fraunces' warmth better than a neutral grotesque. (Acceptable alt: `Hanken Grotesk`.)
- **Arabic (display + body) — `IBM Plex Sans Arabic`** (OFL; IBM + Bold Monday, Arabic script since 2019, 8 weights ExtraLight→Bold). Chosen over Tajawal/Cairo because its proportions, x-height and stroke contrast visually harmonize with the Latin sans at matched optical weight, and it carries both UI and editorial weight credibly — avoiding a second Arabic display face. Numerals are clean Western (Latin) glyphs, which we want.
- **Banned as headline/body:** Inter, Roboto, Arial, system-ui.

> **Script switch is automatic, not manual.** A `font-family` token resolves per active language: Latin stack for fr/en, `IBM Plex Sans Arabic` for ar. Headlines in Arabic use IBM Plex Sans Arabic at heavier weight (700) to echo Fraunces' display presence since there's no single matching Arabic contrast-serif we trust for legibility at scale.

**Loading:**
- **Web (`next/font`):** `next/font/google` for `Fraunces` (axes `opsz,wght` + `italic`) and `Plus_Jakarta_Sans`; `IBM_Plex_Sans_Arabic` with explicit `weight: ['400','500','600','700']` + `subsets:['arabic']`. Each exposes a CSS variable (`--font-display`, `--font-body`, `--font-arabic`) wired into the Tailwind `fontFamily` tokens. `display:'swap'`, self-hosted/zero-layout-shift via `next/font`'s build-time fetch. `<html lang>` + `dir` (see §5) selects the body stack.
- **Expo:** bundle the static `.ttf`/variable files under `assets/fonts`, load with `expo-font` `useFonts()` (or `Font.loadAsync`) behind the splash screen via `expo-splash-screen` so no FOUT. Variable Fraunces is supported on RN New Architecture; fallback ships named static instances (`Fraunces_72pt-Black`, etc.) for safety on older engines.

**Scale** (rem on web / dp on RN — same numeric values; line-heights tuned for serif display vs sans body):

| Token | Size | Line-height | Weight | Face | Use |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `display-xl` | 44 / 56 mobile→web | 1.05 | 600 | Fraunces | hero / landing |
| `display-lg` | 34 | 1.1 | 600 | Fraunces | property title, section hero |
| `heading-1` | 26 | 1.2 | 600 | Fraunces | screen titles |
| `heading-2` | 21 | 1.25 | 600 | Fraunces | card group headers |
| `heading-3` | 18 | 1.3 | 600 | Jakarta | dense headers (sans, not serif) |
| `title` | 17 | 1.35 | 600 | Jakarta | list item title |
| `body-lg` | 16 | 1.55 | 400 | Jakarta | reading text |
| `body` | 15 | 1.5 | 400 | Jakarta | default UI |
| `body-sm` | 13.5 | 1.45 | 400 | Jakarta | secondary |
| `caption` | 12 | 1.4 | 500 | Jakarta | meta, timestamps |
| `overline` | 11 | 1.2 | 600 | Jakarta | labels, +0.06em tracking, uppercase (Latin only — never uppercase Arabic) |
| `price` | 20 | 1.2 | 700 | Jakarta | tabular-nums, see §6 |

Tracking: display sizes −0.01em; Arabic always 0 tracking and never uppercased/`letter-spacing`'d.

---

### 4. Spacing, radii, shadow, z-index, motion

**Spacing** — 4px base, named not numbered in components (`space.md`):

| `xs` 4 · `sm` 8 · `md` 12 · `lg` 16 · `xl` 24 · `2xl` 32 · `3xl` 48 · `4xl` 64 · `5xl` 96 |

**Radii** (soft-editorial, generous but not pill-everything):

| `radius.sm` 8 · `radius.md` 12 · `radius.card` 16 · `radius.lg` 20 · `radius.sheet` 24 (top corners) · `radius.pill` 999 · `radius.full` 9999 |

**Shadows** — teal-tinted, low-opacity, never gray-black (matte editorial look):

| Token | Value |
|---|---|
| `shadow.xs` | `0 1px 2px rgba(14,58,58,.06)` |
| `shadow.card` | `0 2px 8px rgba(14,58,58,.08)` |
| `shadow.raised` | `0 6px 20px rgba(14,58,58,.10)` |
| `shadow.sheet` | `0 -8px 32px rgba(10,42,42,.16)` |
| `shadow.pin` | `0 3px 10px rgba(10,42,42,.30)` (map price pin) |

(RN maps these to `shadow*` iOS props + `elevation` Android; web uses `box-shadow`.)

**Z-index:** `base` 0 · `sticky` 100 (sticky booking widget) · `header` 200 · `dropdown` 300 · `overlay` 1000 · `modal` 1100 · `sheet` 1200 · `toast` 1300.

**Motion** — calm, editorial, never bouncy:

| Durations | `instant` 80 · `fast` 140 · `base` 220 · `slow` 320 · `sheet` 380 (ms) |
| Easings | `standard` `cubic-bezier(.2,0,0,1)` · `decelerate` `cubic-bezier(0,0,0,1)` · `accelerate` `cubic-bezier(.3,0,1,1)` |

Direction-aware: enter/exit translate uses a `dirX` token (+1 LTR / −1 RTL) so slide-in respects reading direction (§5). Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (web) / `AccessibilityInfo.isReduceMotionEnabled` (RN) → fall back to opacity-only.

---

### 5. RTL strategy — full parity, not a flag

Arabic is the **primary** locale, so RTL is the design default mindset, not an afterthought.

- **Logical everything.** Web Tailwind uses logical utilities (`ps-*`/`pe-*`/`ms-*`/`me-*`, `start-*`/`end-*`, `text-start`, `border-s`) — zero `left`/`right` in component CSS. RN uses RN's logical style props (`paddingStart/End`, `marginStart/End`, `start/end`, `textAlign:'left'` resolves per `I18nManager.isRTL`). One stylesheet serves both directions.
- **Web direction:** `<html lang="ar" dir="rtl">` set in the Next.js root layout from the resolved locale (App Router `generateStaticParams` per `[locale]` segment). CSS vars/tokens are direction-agnostic; only `dir` flips flow.
- **Expo direction:** on locale change call `I18nManager.allowRTL(true)` + `I18nManager.forceRTL(isRTL)`, persist the choice, then **hard reload** via `Updates.reloadAsync()` (prod) / dev reload — because RN applies layout direction only at app start and iOS won't reflect it mid-session. The language-switch UI explicitly shows a "applying…" reload state so the restart is intentional UX, not a glitch. (Known Expo Go quirk: `isRTL` can read false in Expo Go; we verify in dev/standalone builds.)
- **Mirrored iconography.** A `<DirectionalIcon>` wrapper flips horizontally-meaningful glyphs (chevron, arrow, back, breadcrumb, send, progress, carousel arrows) via `scaleX(-1)` under RTL. Non-directional icons (heart, star, search, map pin, camera) are **never** mirrored. Icon set defined with an explicit `mirror: true|false` per icon so it's data, not guesswork.
- **Mirrored navigation + motion.** Back gesture, drawer side, tab order, stepper progress, and slide animation direction all key off the `dirX` motion token. Push transitions slide from the leading edge in both directions.
- **Numbers stay Western.** Even in `ar`, prices/phone/dates render with Latin digits (§6) — this is correct for Algeria (`ar-DZ` defaults to Latin digits; we pin it). Arabic text flows RTL; the embedded number runs LTR naturally via the Unicode bidi algorithm — no manual override needed, but price components wrap the numeral in an isolated `<bdi>`/`unicodeBidi:'isolate'` to prevent currency-symbol reordering bugs.

---

### 6. i18next setup (AR / FR / EN)

- **Bundles location:** `packages/i18n/locales/{ar,fr,en}/<namespace>.json`, shipped to both runtimes. Web loads via `i18next-resources-to-backend` (code-split per namespace, SSR-safe with the App Router); Expo bundles JSON statically (no network fetch) and lazy-requires per namespace.
- **Namespaces** (not one mega-file): `common`, `auth`, `search`, `listing`, `booking`, `payment`, `host`, `admin`, `messaging`, `reviews`, `errors`, `validation`, `wilaya` (the 58 wilaya + commune display names — Arabic names are canonical, transliterations for fr/en).
- **Key strategy:** nested, screen/feature-scoped, semantic not literal — `booking.summary.cancellation_policy_flexible`, `listing.amenities.title`, `payment.method.baridi_pay`. Never English-sentence-as-key. ICU-style plural keys (`_one`/`_other` + `_zero`/`_few`/`_many` for Arabic's CLDR plural categories, which i18next supports natively).
- **Detection + persistence:** order = persisted user choice → device locale (`expo-localization` `getLocales()[0]` / `navigator.languages`) → fallback `ar`. Persisted in `AsyncStorage` (Expo) / cookie + `localStorage` (web, cookie so SSR renders the right `dir`/lang on first paint). Logged-in users also store `preferred_locale` on the `User` row in Supabase so locale follows the account across devices; on login we reconcile server value → client store. Fallback chain `ar → fr → en` (so a missing Arabic key shows French before English, matching the Algerian audience).
- **Formatting via `Intl`, wired into i18next's formatter** (`i18next.services.formatter.addCached(...)`), so translators write `{{price, dzd}}` / `{{date, medium}}` inline:
  - **Currency `dzd`:** `new Intl.NumberFormat('${locale}-u-nu-latn', { style:'currency', currency:'DZD', maximumFractionDigits:0 })` → forces **Western Arabic digits** in all locales (Algeria is in the Latin-digit exception group, and `nu-latn` guarantees it), 0 decimals (DZD has no everyday minor unit in v1). We render the symbol as **`دج`** for ar and **`DZD`**/`DA` for fr/en via a thin post-format symbol swap (because `Intl` outputs the ISO `DZD`), keeping numerals grouped per locale.
  - **Number `number`:** `Intl.NumberFormat('${locale}-u-nu-latn')`.
  - **Date/relative:** `Intl.DateTimeFormat` + `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` per locale (`ar` → Gregorian calendar, Arabic month names, Latin digits via `-u-nu-latn`; week starts Saturday for the calendar UI in `ar-DZ`).
- `price` text token always uses `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums` so columns of DZD align.

---

### 7. Shared component inventory (one visual language, two implementations)

> RN and web are **separate implementations** (RN primitives vs. DOM + Tailwind) of the **same tokens + specs**. A Figma library mirrors these names 1:1 so design↔code stays honest. Each component below is specified once (props, states, tokens) and built twice.

| Component | Key spec notes (shared) |
|---|---|
| `Button` | variants `primary`(teal fill) / `accent`(terracotta CTA) / `secondary`(outline) / `ghost` / `destructive`; sizes sm/md/lg; min 44px tap height; loading + disabled states; leading/trailing `DirectionalIcon` |
| `TextField` / `Select` / `Stepper` | `surface` bg, `border-strong` 1px, `radius.md`, focus ring; label + helper + error slot; RTL-aware text align; guest-count stepper |
| `PropertyCard` | photography-forward: 4:3 image carousel, `radius.card`, title (`title`), wilaya·commune meta (`caption`), inline `Rating`, `PriceDisplay` "/ nuit", `FavoriteButton` overlay top-end (flips corner in RTL), `Badge` slot for trust marks |
| `Rating` | terracotta stars (`rating-star`), numeric `4.8` + count; half-star; aria/label "4.8 ${t('reviews.out_of_5')}" |
| `PriceDisplay` | tabular-nums, `bdi`-isolated numeral, "from"/"à partir de"/"ابتداءً من" prefix, "/ night" suffix, optional strikethrough original |
| `DateRangeCalendar` | range select, min-nights, disabled/blocked from `Availability`; RTL month flow; Saturday-start in ar-DZ; price-per-night dots |
| `BookingWidget` | **sticky** (web: `position:sticky` z`sticky`; RN: pinned bottom bar) — dates, guests, price breakdown, total in DZD, primary `accent` CTA "Réserver / احجز" |
| `Badge` / `TrustMark` | status (`approved`/`pending`/`suspended` mapped to success/warning/error-bg), "Superhost"-style host badge, "Vérifié" verified-host mark, payment-accepted chips (Chargily/Baridi) |
| `Avatar` / `HostChip` | initials fallback, verified tick |
| `Skeleton` | `surface-sunken` base, shimmer respecting reduced-motion; card/list/detail variants |
| `EmptyState` / `ErrorState` | illustration slot, title (`heading-2`, Fraunces), body, action button; offline + retry variant |
| `Toast` / `InlineAlert` | status-colored bg + earthy fg; auto-dismiss; RTL icon side |
| `MapPricePin` | rounded teal pill, `shadow.pin`, terracotta when selected/active, DZD label tabular-nums, pointer tail; clustered count variant |
| `Sheet` / `Modal` | bottom sheet (`radius.sheet`, `shadow.sheet`) on mobile, centered modal on web; teal-tinted `overlay` scrim |
| `SegmentedControl` / `Tabs` / `FilterChip` | active = accent underline/fill; RTL order + animated indicator direction |

---

### 8. Anti-slop + accessibility checklist (graded — concrete rules)

**Anti-AI-slop (visual discipline):**
- Accent (terracotta) appears on **≤1 primary action per view**; if everything's terracotta, nothing is. Teal is structure, sand/bone is ground, photos are color.
- No decorative gradients, no glass blur, no neon, no drop-shadow on flat UI; shadows are teal-tinted and subtle (§4) only on genuinely floating surfaces.
- Headlines are Fraunces (serif), not the body sans bolded — the type pairing must be *visible*. Body is never justified; Arabic is never uppercased, letter-spaced, or faux-bolded.
- Rating stars are terracotta, not yellow; status colors are earthy, not stock web red/green — palette stays coherent with the brand.
- Real Algerian content in mockups: wilaya names (Tlemcen, Béjaïa, Ghardaïa, Tamanrasset), DZD prices with Latin digits, three-language toggle shown working.

**Accessibility (hard rules):**
- **Contrast:** body text `ink-900` on `bone-200`/surface ≥ 7:1 (AAA); `text-muted` `ink-500` ≥ 4.5:1; text-on-primary `bone-100` on `teal-800` ≥ 4.5:1; text-on-accent verified ≥ 4.5:1 (use `ink-900` on light terracotta states, `bone-100` on `terracotta-700`). Status text always paired with its `*-bg`, never color-only signaling (icon + text too).
- **Tap targets:** ≥ 44×44 pt (RN `hitSlop` where visual is smaller); ≥ 8px between adjacent targets.
- **Focus:** visible 2px `focus-ring` (terracotta) + 2px offset on every interactive element (web `:focus-visible`; RN focus state on TV/keyboard); never `outline:none` without replacement.
- **Screen readers:** every icon-only button has a localized `accessibilityLabel`/`aria-label` (sourced from i18n, so it's translated); images from hosts require `alt`; `PriceDisplay` announces full "12 000 dinars par nuit", not "12000 دج" glyph-by-glyph; live regions for booking/payment status (`aria-live` / `AccessibilityInfo.announceForAccessibility`).
- **Forms:** label always present (no placeholder-as-label); errors associated (`aria-describedby` / RN `accessibilityHint`) and not color-only; `inputMode`/keyboard type per field (numeric for guest count, tel for phone).
- **RTL parity is an a11y requirement:** every screen QA'd in ar/fr/en; no clipped Arabic, no LTR-stuck layouts, mirrored back/chevron verified; text scales to 200% (web) / Dynamic Type (iOS) / font-scale (Android) without truncation.
- **Reduced motion** honored everywhere (§4); no autoplay carousels without pause.

**Sources:** [Fraunces — Google Fonts](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Fraunces) · [Fraunces (Google Design)](https://design.google/library/a-new-take-on-old-style-typeface) · [IBM Plex Sans Arabic — Google Fonts](https://fonts.google.com/specimen/IBM+Plex+Sans+Arabic) · [IBM Plex license/repo](https://github.com/IBM/plex) · [Expo Localization docs](https://docs.expo.dev/guides/localization/) · [Expo RTL restart behavior (issue #23629)](https://github.com/expo/expo/issues/23629) · [i18next formatting](https://www.i18next.com/translation-function/formatting) · [Intl.NumberFormat — MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Intl/NumberFormat) · [Arabic numerals by region (ar-DZ uses Latin digits)](https://helw.net/2025/05/31/arabic-numbers-and-regions/)
